atomic_nodes.content: 1.0 SCOPE

This FM property loss prevention data sheet contains recommendations for the installation of automatic sprinkler systems that consist of automatic sprinklers and their aboveground supporting components.
This data sheet provides guidance on the following:
Components used as part of a sprinkler system
Securing and supporting these components
The response time of sprinklers to a fire
The distribution of sprinkler discharge to a fire area
The documentation required for an FM plan review
The information required for an FM acceptance test
This data sheet does not provide guidance on:
Designs for ceiling or in-rack sprinkler systems (see the relevant occupancy-specific data sheet for design guidelines).
This data sheet does, however, provide design information for supplemental sprinklers.
In addition, this data sheet provides the minimum recommended design pressures for ceiling and in-rack sprinklers.
Maintenance required for sprinkler systems (see Data Sheet 2-81, Fire Safety Inspections and Sprinkler System Maintenance)
Underground piping or water supplies for sprinkler systems (see Data Sheet 3-10, Private Fire Service Mains and Connections, and other relevant 3-series data sheets)
Protection of sprinkler system piping from internal corrosion (see Data Sheet 2-1, Corrosion in Automatic Sprinkler Systems)

Designs for the installation of piping in areas designated as 50-year through 500-year earthquake zones as defined in Data Sheet 1-2, Earthquakes (see Data Sheet 2-8, Earthquake Protection for Water-Based Fire Protection Systems)
1.1 Hazards
Refer to the following FM Understanding the Hazard (UTH) brochures for information on hazards related to sprinkler installations:
Corrosion in Sprinkler Systems (P0109)
Freeze (P0148)
Ice Plugs (P0118)
Ice Plugs in Dry-Pendent Sprinklers in Freezers (P0382)
Inadequate Ceiling Sprinkler Protection in Storage Facilities (P253)
Lack of Automatic Sprinklers (P0037)
Storage Sprinkler Installation Issues (P0477)
1.2 Changes
April 2025. Interim revision. The following modifications were incorporated into this revision:
A. The guidelines for when sprinkler protection is recommended within ceiling pockets has been updated.
B. Editorial changes have been introduced to better clarify the recommendations offered in this data sheet.
1.3 Superseded Information
This data sheet incorporates and supersedes Data Sheet 8-29, Refrigerated Storage.
This data sheet incorporates and supersedes the following public engineering bulletins:
EB 08-05, Use of Smoke Detection to Activate a Refrigerated Area Sprinkler System
EB 02-11, High-Volume Low-Speed (HVLS) Fan and Sprinkler Performance
2.0 LOSS PREVENTION RECOMMENDATIONS

atomic_nodes.content: Use this flowchart in combination with the text of this data sheet to determine the recommendations for the installation of an automatic sprinkler system.

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: 2.1.2 Where Sprinklers Are Needed

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: 2.1.2.1 Install sprinklers throughout all portions of a building in accordance with this data sheet to protect it from fire damage.

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: 2.1.2.2 Sprinkler protection can be omitted from portions of a building in which both the construction and the occupancy of the affected building area are noncombustible.

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: 2.1.2.3 See the relevant occupancy-specific data sheet for design criteria (i.e., sprinkler system design, hose stream allowance, and sprinkler system duration) for the sprinkler system.

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: Determine if there are any specific recommendations in the occupancy-specific data sheet that might supersede the general recommendations given in this data sheet.

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: 2.1.2.4 Fixed special protection extinguishing systems, including oxygen reduction systems, are not an alternative to sprinkler protection unless the relevant occupancy-specific data sheet specifies they are.

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: 2.1.3 Water Supplies for Sprinkler Systems

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: 2.1.3.1 Provide each sprinkler system with at least one reliable water supply capable of meeting the total flow and pressure demand (ceiling, in-rack, and hose stream), and duration for the occupancy hazard the sprinkler system is designed to handle.

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: See additional information in Data Sheet 3-29, Reliability of Fire Protection Water Supplies.

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: 2.1.3.2 Arrange the piping network for the water supply in accordance with Data Sheet 3-10, Private Fire Service Mains and Connections.

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: 2.1.3.3 See other 3-series data sheets for additional information regarding the type of water supply being provided.

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: Applicable data sheets, in addition to Data Sheet 3-29 and Data Sheet 3-10, could include the following:

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: Data Sheet 3-1, Tanks and Reservoirs for Interconnected Fire Service and Public Mains

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: Data Sheet 3-2, Water Tanks for Fire Protection

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: Data Sheet 3-4, Embankment-Supported Fabric Tanks

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: Data Sheet 3-6, Lined Earth Reservoirs for Fire Protection

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: Data Sheet 3-7, Fire Protection Pumps

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: 2.1.3.4 The water supply for domestic use can tap off the water supply for a sprinkler system if the feed connection for the domestic supply is upstream of both:

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: A. The waterflow alarm on the sprinkler system riser, and

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: B. The fire service connection.

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: 2.1.3.5 Prior to making the connection to the sprinkler system, completely flush all underground mains and lead-in connections that feed sprinkler system risers until the water runs clear.

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: Flushing can be accomplished through hydrants at dead ends of the system or through accessible aboveground flushing outlets.

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: If water is supplied from more than one source or from a looped system, close divisional valves as needed to produce a high-velocity flow through each single leg of the underground's network of piping.

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: Acceptable minimum flushing flow rates are as follows:

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: A. The hydraulically calculated water demand rate of the system including any hose requirements, or

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: B. The flow necessary to provide a velocity of 10 ft per sec (3 m/sec) as indicated in Table 2.1.3.5, or

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: C. The maximum flow rate available to the system under fire conditions.

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
2.1.4 General Recommendations for Sprinkler Systems and their Components
atomic_nodes.content: 2.1.4 General Recommendations for Sprinkler Systems and their Components

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
2.1.4 General Recommendations for Sprinkler Systems and their Components
atomic_nodes.content: 2.1.4.1 Install only new, FM Approved (where available), sprinkler system components.

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
2.1.4 General Recommendations for Sprinkler Systems and their Components
atomic_nodes.content: 2.1.4.2 Ensure the application of each component is in accordance with the following:

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
2.1.4 General Recommendations for Sprinkler Systems and their Components
atomic_nodes.content: A. Its listing in the Approval Guide, and

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
2.1.4 General Recommendations for Sprinkler Systems and their Components
atomic_nodes.content: B. The manufacturer's installation instructions, and

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
2.1.4 General Recommendations for Sprinkler Systems and their Components
atomic_nodes.content: C. The relevant occupancy-specific data sheet, and

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
2.1.4 General Recommendations for Sprinkler Systems and their Components
atomic_nodes.content: D. Any specific jurisdictional requirements

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
2.1.4 General Recommendations for Sprinkler Systems and their Components
atomic_nodes.content: 2.1.4.3 Ensure the sprinkler system components are compatible with each other as well as the environment in which they will be installed, including the highest expected internal pressure, and the lowest and highest ambient temperatures.

2.1.4 General Recommendations for Sprinkler Systems and their Components

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: 2.1.5 Other Relevant Data Sheets

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: In addition to the guidelines in this data sheet, see the following data sheets for potential additional guidelines that could affect the installation and design of automatic sprinkler systems:

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: Data Sheet 1-20, Protection Against Exterior Fire Exposure, for recommendations related to installing exposure sprinklers for the protection of exterior hazards, such as large oil-filled transformers, exterior loading docks, yard storage, etc. that are close to a building that either has or requires sprinkler protection

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: Data Sheet 1-57, Plastics in Construction, to determine what supplemental protection related to sprinkler protection might be recommended when plastic construction materials are introduced into the area to be protected

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: Data Sheet 2-1, Corrosion in Automatic Sprinkler Systems, for recommendations on how to prevent internal corrosion in sprinkler system piping when sprinkler system components will be installed in atypical environments, such as corrosive, low-temperature, or high-temperature type of environments

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: Data Sheet 2-8, Earthquake Protection for Water-Based Fire Protection Systems, for sprinkler systems that will be installed in 50-year through 500-year earthquake zones (as defined by Data Sheet 1-2, Earthquakes)

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: Data Sheet 2-81, Fire Safety Inspections and Sprinkler System Maintenance, regarding the recommendations on how to care for a sprinkler system once it has been installed

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: Data Sheet 3-26, Fire Protection for Nonstorage Occupancies, for the sprinkler system designs of most nonstorage types of occupancy hazards

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: Data Sheet 8-1, Commodity Classification, for the classification of most products that might be found in a general warehouse environment

breadcrumbs: 2.1 General Recommendations for the Installation of Sprinklers
atomic_nodes.content: Data Sheet 8-9, Storage of Class 1, 2, 3, 4 and Plastic Commodities, for the sprinkler system designs of most storage occupancy hazards
2.2 Sprinkler System Types

breadcrumbs: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types
atomic_nodes.content: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types

breadcrumbs: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types
atomic_nodes.content: 2.2.1.1 Selection of Sprinkler System

breadcrumbs: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types
atomic_nodes.content: 2.2.1.1.1 See the relevant occupancy-specific data sheet to determine if there are any restrictions on the types of sprinkler systems available for installation.

breadcrumbs: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types
atomic_nodes.content: 2.2.1.1.2 When the relevant occupancy-specific data sheet recommends a specific sprinkler system type for installation, see the applicable subsection in Section 2.2 for recommendations pertaining to that sprinkler system.

breadcrumbs: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types
atomic_nodes.content: 2.2.1.1.3 When the occupancy-specific data sheet does not recommend a specific sprinkler system type for installation, see Sections 2.2.2 through 2.2.9 to determine which sprinkler systems are applicable for the area to be protected.

breadcrumbs: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types
2.2.1.2 Compatibility of Sprinkler System Components with the Environment
atomic_nodes.content: 2.2.1.2 Compatibility of Sprinkler System Components with the Environment

breadcrumbs: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types
2.2.1.2 Compatibility of Sprinkler System Components with the Environment
atomic_nodes.content: 2.2.1.2.1 Ensure the materials chosen for the sprinkler system are compatible with the environment they will be protecting.

breadcrumbs: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types
2.2.1.2 Compatibility of Sprinkler System Components with the Environment
atomic_nodes.content: See the Approval Guide for sprinkler system components that can be used in atypical environments, such as corrosive, low- or high-temperature environments.

breadcrumbs: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types
2.2.1.3 Recommended Sprinkler System Design and Hydraulic Calculations
atomic_nodes.content: 2.2.1.3 Recommended Sprinkler System Design and Hydraulic Calculations

breadcrumbs: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types
2.2.1.3 Recommended Sprinkler System Design and Hydraulic Calculations
atomic_nodes.content: 2.2.1.3.1 See the relevant occupancy-specific data sheet for recommendations regarding the sprinkler system design for the area to be protected.

breadcrumbs: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types
2.2.1.3 Recommended Sprinkler System Design and Hydraulic Calculations
atomic_nodes.content: 2.2.1.3.2 See Data Sheet 3-0, Hydraulics of Fire Protection Systems, for information related to the hydraulic calculation of a sprinkler system.

breadcrumbs: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types
2.2.1.3 Recommended Sprinkler System Design and Hydraulic Calculations
atomic_nodes.content: 2.2.1.3.3 When the occupancy-specific data sheet does not provide guidance on the Hazen-Williams C-Value (roughness coefficient) to be used for hydraulic analysis of the sprinkler system, see the default values given in Table 2.2.1.3.3.

breadcrumbs: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types
2.2.1.3 Recommended Sprinkler System Design and Hydraulic Calculations
atomic_nodes.content: 2.2.1.3.4 The default Hazen-Williams C-Value for a vacuum sprinkler system can be 120 when the sprinkler piping arrangement of the system meets all the following conditions:

breadcrumbs: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types
2.2.1.3 Recommended Sprinkler System Design and Hydraulic Calculations
atomic_nodes.content: A. There is single-path flow (i.e., no loops or grids) throughout the sprinkler system, and

breadcrumbs: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types
2.2.1.3 Recommended Sprinkler System Design and Hydraulic Calculations
atomic_nodes.content: B. When applicable, the water supply can meet the maximum recommended water delivery time, and

breadcrumbs: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types
2.2.1.3 Recommended Sprinkler System Design and Hydraulic Calculations
atomic_nodes.content: C. The recommendations in Section 2.2.1.4 are satisfied, and

breadcrumbs: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types
2.2.1.3 Recommended Sprinkler System Design and Hydraulic Calculations
atomic_nodes.content: D. The pipe connection method avoids the collection of water, and

breadcrumbs: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types
2.2.1.3 Recommended Sprinkler System Design and Hydraulic Calculations
atomic_nodes.content: E. The sprinkler branch lines and mains are pitched to avoid water accumulation within the sprinkler system.

breadcrumbs: 2.2.1 General Recommendations for Sprinkler System Types
2.2.1.3 Recommended Sprinkler System Design and Hydraulic Calculations
atomic_nodes.content: 2.2.1.3.5 Post the sprinkler system's design criteria on a rigid placard installed at the system valve.

2.2.1.3 Recommended Sprinkler System Design and Hydraulic Calculations
